summary: Iteration 51 - Backend testing for Web Push VAPID feature. All 21 tests passed (100% success rate). Features verified: VAPID public key endpoint, push subscription CRUD (subscribe/unsubscribe/status), test push endpoint, alert creation with push hook, and regression tests for existing routers.

web_push_vapid_endpoints: GET /api/push/vapid-public-key: Returns 200 with public_key field (VAPID key verified)
POST /api/push/subscribe: Creates subscription with valid payload (endpoint + keys)
POST /api/push/subscribe idempotency: Calling twice with same endpoint doesn't create duplicate (upsert works)
POST /api/push/subscribe without auth: Returns 403 (correctly rejects unauthenticated)
POST /api/push/subscribe with invalid payload: Returns 400 (missing keys rejected)
GET /api/push/status: Returns configured:true and active_subscriptions count
GET /api/push/status without auth: Returns 403 (correctly rejects unauthenticated)
POST /api/push/test with no subscriptions: Returns sent:0 (no error)
POST /api/push/test with fake subscription: Handles gracefully without 500 (pywebpush error swallowed)
alert_creation_with_push: POST /api/alerts with severity=critical: Creates alert successfully, push hook doesn't break flow
regression_routers: POST /api/ingest/syslog: Endpoint exists (returns 422 for empty payload, not 404)

critical_code_review_comments: Web Push VAPID implementation is complete and well-structured
webpush.py: is_configured() properly checks both VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY and VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY
webpush.py: _send_one() handles WebPushException gracefully, marks expired subscriptions (404/410)
webpush.py: notify_new_alert() is fire-and-forget with try/except, won't block alert ingestion
routes/push.py: All 5 endpoints implemented correctly with proper auth (get_current_user)
routes/push.py: subscribe uses upsert pattern to avoid duplicates
routes/alerts.py: Push hook at line 57-61 is properly wrapped in try/except
server.py: push_router correctly imported and included at lines 203 and 237
VAPID keys are properly set in backend/.env

bugs_fixed_during_testing.issue: AlertResponse() got multiple values for keyword argument 'id' when handling duplicate alerts
bugs_fixed_during_testing.fix: Changed to create a copy of alert_doc and override id with original_id before passing to AlertResponse

seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up test push subscriptions during testing (unique endpoints per test run)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 51 verified Web Push VAPID feature. All /api/push/* endpoints work correctly: vapid-public-key returns the configured key, subscribe/unsubscribe manage push_subscriptions collection with upsert pattern, status returns subscription count, test sends push to user's subscriptions. The push notification hook in alerts.py (line 57-61) and ingestion.py is wrapped in try/except so it won't break alert creation even if push fails. Fixed a pre-existing bug in alerts.py where duplicate alert handling passed 'id' twice to AlertResponse. pywebpush==2.3.0 is installed and working.
